_meta.description: KEX2 endoprotease cleavage rules for the Ward 1995 glucoamylase-KEX2-payload fusion architecture. Validated in A. oryzae by Huynh et al. 2020 (PMC7257131) for adalimumab heavy+light chain dual-cassette.
_meta.enzyme: Kex2p-family endoprotease (furin-like subtilisin, calcium-dependent serine protease). A. oryzae encodes a KEX2 ortholog (gene: kexB; AO090701000432); the Huynh 2020 KRGGG linker was cleaved by this endogenous enzyme.
sources: Bathurst IC et al. Science 1987;235:348-50 — KEX2 cleavage rule KR|X (PMID 3099383)
Rockwell NC, Krysan DJ, Komiyama T, Fuller RS. Chem Rev 2002;102(12):4525-48 — P1' preferences comprehensive review (PMID 12475198)
Brenner C, Fuller RS. Proc Natl Acad Sci 1992;89:922-6 — P1' restrictions: D/E abolished; P reduced; preferred small/uncharged (PMID 1371243)
Huynh HH et al. Fungal Biol Biotechnol 2020;7:7 — KRGGG linker geometry validated in A. oryzae dual-cassette (PMC7257131)
Ward PP et al. Biotechnology (N Y) 1995;13:498-503 — original glucoamylase-KR-hLf architecture (PMID 9634791)

cleavage_rule.p2: K (Lys) strongly preferred; R (Arg) tolerated at P2

p1_prime_preferences.notes: P1'=P (Pro) dramatically reduces cleavage rate (rigid backbone constrains Kex2 active site). P1'=D or E (acidic) abolishes cleavage in the canonical active site geometry. P1'=K or R (basic) reduces efficiency but does not abolish. Gly (G) is the best-characterized compatible small residue — hence KRGGG linker.

huynh_2020_validated_linker.additional_gly_rationale: GGG spacer provides conformational flexibility between glucoamylase C-terminus and payload N-terminus; allows Kex2 active site to access the KR dipeptide without steric clash from folded payload domain. Three Gly residues chosen empirically in Ward 1995; validated by Huynh 2020 for two simultaneous cassettes.
huynh_2020_validated_linker.context: AmyB signal peptide — glucoamylase propeptide — glucoamylase catalytic domain — KRGGG — [payload] — TamyB terminator

internal_kr_site_risk.description: Internal K-R dipeptides within the payload sequence (downstream of the KEX2 linker) will be recognized and cleaved by endogenous Kex2p, producing truncated proteins. This is the primary Ward-architecture failure mode for new payloads.

risk_categories.low: 0 internal K-R dipeptides in mature payload sequence
risk_categories.moderate: 1-2 internal K-R dipeptides; may produce minor truncation bands visible on SDS-PAGE
risk_categories.high: 3+ internal K-R dipeptides, or any K-R in an exposed flexible loop (high cleavage probability)

internal_kr_site_risk.p1_prime_modifier: For each internal K-R site, check the P1' residue. If P1'=D or E: near-zero cleavage risk (site is non-functional). If P1'=P: low risk. If P1'=G/A/S: high risk (site is likely cleaved). Adjust risk downward for sites buried in well-folded domains (pLDDT > 80 at P1 and flanking residues).

alternative_linkers.description: If internal K-R sites in a payload are problematic, alternative linker sequences reduce recognition ambiguity while preserving cleavage at the designed site.

options.notes: Thrombin recognition site; not native to Kex2p; requires exogenous protease. Not compatible with in vivo Kex2p-based processing.
Extended Gly linker — adds conformational flexibility but same recognition dipeptide. No published evidence of improved specificity over KRGGG.
P2=R reduces Kex2 efficiency ~2-3x relative to P2=K. Useful if internal K-R sites dominate and K-containing linkers worsen off-target cleavage.
